target.name: Intramotev

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Intramotev is a Series A–stage autonomous systems company founded by Tim Luchini, Alex Peiffer, and Corey Vasel. The valuation universe comprises eight private peers in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker. No public comparables exist in this subsector.

A traditional peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints; the broader peer set lacked the concentration needed for reliable triangulation and would risk anchoring to mismatched risk profiles. Stage-progression analysis, however, is applicable. Using total capital raised of $14.4M as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A–to–Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $29M. This method reflects observed progression patterns across frontier-tech financings but assumes Intramotev follows standard dilution and milestone-achievement cadences.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $24M to $33M, with a median point of $29M. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-company benchmarks at identical stage and valuation tier, which limits external validation of the step-up multiple. Sensitivity to the 2.0x multiplier is material; a 1.7x multiplier would imply $24M, while 2.3x would suggest $33M. Resolution of product-stage maturity, capital efficiency, and regulatory pathway clarity would refine this range in future rounds.
